verification.note: Reported by the WirelessJEPA authors (Chu, Mashaal, Abou-Zeid, 'WirelessJEPA: A Multi-Antenna Foundation Model using Spatio-temporal Wireless Latent Predictions,' arXiv:2601.20190, submitted 28 Jan 2026), NOT reproduced or re-run by this repo. Table II ('Linear vs. k-NN accuracy (%)'), row 'Wi-Fi Prtcl.' under 'Out-of-domain (Transfer)': Linear = 94.26%, k-NN = 84.89%, both at 500-shot (Section IV / Fig. 3 caption: 'linear probing accuracy at 500-shots on five OOD downstream tasks'). Both figures confirmed verbatim from the raw arXiv HTML (single occurrence of each number in the results table). We cite only the linear-probe figure (94.26%) as accuracy_overall; the k-NN figure (84.89%) is a secondary non-parametric-classifier number from the same table, not this task's primary metric, and is not stored as a separate metrics.values key. DATASET: stronger match than initially assumed -- Section III-D's downstream-task list reads verbatim: 'WiFi (4-Class) [13]: OTA classification of IEEE 802.11ax/b/n/g protocols using (2,1024) raw I/Q windows extracted from Room A captures.' Reference [13] is Belgiovine et al., 'T-PRIME: Transformer-based protocol identification for machine learning at the edge,' IEEE INFOCOM 2024 -- the SAME paper backing our board's `tprime`/`tprime-paper` rows, and the paper reuses T-PRIME's own 'Room A' room-naming convention. This is strong internal evidence the WiFi 4-class IQ windows are (a room-split subset of) T-PRIME's own released Northeastern OTA capture (neu:h989s8519), not an independent private testbed -- but the paper never states 'we used T-PRIME's released dataset' verbatim, so this remains an inference, not a confirmed identity. SPLIT/PROTOCOL MISMATCH (why still _uncertain, not from_paper): our canonical split proto-tprime-wifi4-8010-seed42-v1 is a locally-generated random 80/10/10 stratified split (seed 42) over the full dataset, structurally different from WirelessJEPA's 500-shot linear-probe/k-NN train/eval carve-out used to produce Table II -- even if the underlying capture is the same, sample-level overlap between our test split and WirelessJEPA's probe/eval carve-out is not established. EVAL-REGIME MISMATCH: our primary metric accuracy_overall is a full-test-set from_scratch-style evaluation; the paper's 94.26% is a frozen-encoder 500-shot linear-probe figure (regime declared here as few_shot/k_shot=500 to avoid conflating regimes, matching leaderboard/results/interference_id/wirelessjepa_paper.json and leaderboard/results/amc/wirelessjepa_paper.json for the same paper). Given a real committed canonical split to cite, verbatim-confirmed figures, and direct in-repo precedent for this exact tiering shape (tprime_paper.json in this same task directory, and wirelessjepa_paper.json in interference_id), this row is tiered from_paper_uncertain: addable as a literature reference point, but not directly comparable to the self_reported `tprime` row (accuracy_overall 0.995, from_scratch, full test set) or to `tprime-paper` (from_paper_uncertain, T-PRIME's own 0.99 time-split figure) on this board.
